cases.title: One surgery, billed as two (duplicate facility fee)
cases.persona: Insured, in-network. Had one outpatient surgery, itemized bill shows two identical OR charges. Confused and upset; not a billing expert.

turns.text: I had a single outpatient surgery last month — I was only in the operating room once. But my itemized bill shows two charges that look exactly the same. Can you help me understand what's going on, and whether I actually owe all of this?

doc.name: itemized-statement.pdf
lines: Patient: Jamie H.    Date of service: 03/14/2026

turns.text: So what should I actually do next?

cases.title: Out-of-network ground ambulance balance bill after a crash
cases.persona: Insured. In a car crash, took an ambulance, hit with a surprise out-of-network balance bill ~3x what their siblings paid for the same ride. Feels it's unfair; had no choice.

turns.text: I was in a car accident and an ambulance took me to the ER. My insurance paid part of it but now I'm being billed $2,711 more. My two siblings were in the same crash, took different ambulances, and paid way less than me for basically the same thing. Is this even allowed? Do I have to pay it?
What are my options to actually fight this or bring the cost down?

cases.title: Sold as a free matching call, billed as a clinical session ($179, never delivered)
cases.persona: Insured. A teletherapy 'matching/onboarding' call was billed to insurance as a clinical session against the deductible; no therapist ever delivered. Provider refuses to refund, claiming it's 'legally unable to.'

turns.text: I signed up with a teletherapy company. They had me do a 45-minute 'matching call' to pair me with a therapist — nobody told me it would cost anything. Three weeks later there's still no therapist, and now there's a $179 charge on my insurance for a 'clinical session.' They're refusing to refund me, saying they're 'legally unable to.' What can I do here?

doc.name: claim-detail.pdf
lines: TWO CHAIRS - CLAIM DETAIL (from insurer portal)
Member: Pedro C.    Date of service: 05/12/2026

turns.text: They keep rotating me to different support reps and nobody gives me a straight answer. Is 'we're legally unable to refund' even a real thing?
